Module 3: Seed Launch, Part 2
!
Executive Summary
!
The next step in your Seed Launch is to decide what type of offer you’re
going to create. You do not have to do all this - a lot of it is just for
brainstorming purposes.
You’ll see a bunch of different methods and choices, go through the options
and pick and choose. Teleseminars or webinars are a key part of seed
launches.
The key to this is surveying people before and after…
• 	Example: “Hey folks, last call was about tuning your guitar. What
questions do you still have about this?”
Then mention the next topic and have them provide feedback on it.
Also, doing it live usually provides more value vs. a pre-recorded video.
You can bring on guest experts when you don’t have knowledge on a topic.
This could also be used as a bonus, and would be done as an interview on
a live call or pre-recorded video.
!
Types of Offers
Hot Seats: if there is work involved you can have students turn in their
work and get live feedback
Example: Learning how to write a resume, they send it in and you
provide feedback.
!

-- 1 of 3 --

Case Studies: These are after the fact results a student got from your
training
Question and Answer Calls
One-on-one calls: additional value where you coach people individually
Recordings: these should be offered because not everyone will show up
for the calls
Transcripts: take recordings and get them transcribed
May require some editing to make them sound more conversational
Crazy Thought: Live Workshop - this would increase the value and
therefore the price
A live environment - anywhere from 10-30 people
If you have a business with a 1000+ person list, you could do this
style to get a higher price
!
Something to keep in mind is that most Seed Launches are done with a
small list and would be webinar based.
• 	Example: Product Launch Manager was a live event with 50 people at
$25,000 each. Took the recordings and turned that into a home study
course - resulted in a multi-million dollar launch.
!
!
!
!
!
!
!

-- 2 of 3 --

Pricing
Depends on your market
High price builds value for better and higher priced future products
Higher price usually means higher compliance
Most important thing is to fill the class - you can sell some but comp
others
Could do entire event for free but not ideal - less compliance and
perceived value
!
Action Steps
Design your Seed Launch offer
Choose one main method to deliver your product (e.g. live event,
webinar)
Add high value pieces to make the offer more enticing (e.g. recordings,
coaching, etc.)
Add bonus(es) to create more value
Finalize structure and sequence
Decide on your pricing
!
